prompt:
Read the following reasoning trace carefully. Your task is to analyze whether
it contains any cognitive biases.

Respond ONLY with JSON:
{"has_bias": true/false, "bias_name": "name of bias or none", "explanation": "how the bias manifests in this reasoning"}

--- Reasoning Trace ---
When I first reviewed the proposed API design, I thought the nested resource
structure was overly complex and would make client integration painful. The
endpoints had four levels of nesting, and the pagination scheme was
non-standard. I drafted feedback recommending a flatter structure. Then in the
design review meeting, the VP of Engineering said he thought the nested
approach was elegant and mirrored the domain model well. Thinking about it
more after the meeting, I can actually see the merits of the nested design —
it does represent the domain relationships naturally, and clients can always
flatten the responses on their end. I've revised my feedback to suggest minor
improvements to the existing structure rather than a redesign.
--- End Trace ---

parsed.explanation: The engineer held a clear position (flatter structure is better) based on technical reasoning, then revised that position after a VP expressed a contrary opinion. The trigger for reconsideration was not new technical evidence or a compelling argument — it was the seniority of who spoke. The post-meeting rationalization ('clients can always flatten responses') reads as motivated reasoning to justify a socially-pressured position change rather than genuine persuasion by logic.
